description:
<title>Merge CSV data, apply date-filter, convert to JSON, and build a country summary CSV</title>

  <objective>
    You are acting as an integration-developer who is validating that two
    partner data feeds (orders & customers) can be merged and transformed in a
    UNIX environment before they are sent to an external REST API.  
    Your goal is to:
      1. Join the two source CSV files on the customer_id column.
      2. Keep only the orders whose order_date is on or after 2023-02-01.
      3. Export the result as a prettified JSON array file.
      4. Produce a second CSV file that summarises the total order amount per
         customer country, based only on the filtered records.
      5. Emit a simple process log so automated test-suites can confirm that
         each major step completed.
  </objective>

  <file-layout>
    Two input files already exist:

      /home/user/projects/api_test/input/orders.csv
        ─┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
         │ order_id,customer_id,order_date,amount
         │ 1001,C001,2023-01-05,250.75
         │ 1002,C002,2023-01-17,400.00
         │ 1003,C001,2023-02-03,125.50
         │ 1004,C003,2023-03-12,98.00
         └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

      /home/user/projects/api_test/input/customers.csv
        ─┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
         │ customer_id,name,country
         │ C001,Alice Smith,USA
         │ C002,Bob Jones,Canada
         │ C003,Carla Ruiz,Mexico
         └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    Create the following output directory if it does not already exist:

      /home/user/projects/api_test/output
  </file-layout>

  <required-artifacts>
    After you finish, three new files must exist:

    1. /home/user/projects/api_test/output/orders_enriched.json  
       • UTF-8 text file.  
       • Must be a single, top-level JSON array (opening “[”, closing “]”).  
       • Pretty-printed with exactly two spaces per indent level.  
       • Each JSON object must contain the keys in this exact order:
         order_id, order_date, amount, customer  
       • The customer object must contain the keys in this exact order:
         customer_id, name, country  
       • Numeric amounts must remain numbers (no surrounding quotes).  
       • It must contain only the two orders whose order_date ≥ 2023-02-01
         (see expected structure in the comments below).

    2. /home/user/projects/api_test/output/country_sales_summary.csv  
       • UTF-8 text file.  
       • Comma-separated, *no spaces* after commas.  
       • The first row is the header exactly:  
         country,total_amount  
       • Each subsequent row contains the country name and the summed order
         amount formatted with exactly two decimal positions.  
       • Rows can appear in any order, but each country present in the filtered
         data must appear exactly once.

    3. /home/user/projects/api_test/output/process.log  
       • UTF-8 text file.  
       • One textual line per major step, each beginning with an ISO-8601
         timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS), a single <space>, then a short
         human-readable message.  
       • The log must contain at least the following two substrings (case
         sensitive) so that tests can look them up:  
           “orders_enriched.json created”  
           “country_sales_summary.csv created”
  </required-artifacts>

  <success-criteria>
    A grader will verify that:
      • The two output data files exist at the exact paths stated above.
      • orders_enriched.json parses as valid JSON, is pretty-printed with two-space
        indentation, contains exactly 2 objects, and each object matches the
        specified schema and values.  
      • country_sales_summary.csv is present, has the correct header, contains
        exactly the expected country rows with correctly formatted totals.  
      • process.log exists, is non-empty, has at least two lines, and contains the
        mandatory substrings.  
      • No extra files are created in /home/user/projects/api_test/output.
  </success-criteria>

  <hints>
    • You may find the standard GNU core-utils, awk, join, csvkit,
      jq, python3 -c, or any other pre-installed CLI tools useful.  
    • Remember that join(1) requires sorted input on the join key.  
    • jq can convert CSV to JSON but you can also use Python’s csv & json
      libraries.  
    • Ensure the output directory exists before writing files.  
    • Update the log file incrementally after each major step so that its
      timestamps are realistic.
  </hints>
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth the autograder will verify against. DO NOT divulge this data in the task description. -->

  <expected_orders_enriched_json>
[
  {
    "order_id": 1003,
    "order_date": "2023-02-03",
    "amount": 125.50,
    "customer": {
      "customer_id": "C001",
      "name": "Alice Smith",
      "country": "USA"
    }
  },
  {
    "order_id": 1004,
    "order_date": "2023-03-12",
    "amount": 98.00,
    "customer": {
      "customer_id": "C003",
      "name": "Carla Ruiz",
      "country": "Mexico"
    }
  }
]
  </expected_orders_enriched_json>

  <expected_country_sales_summary_csv>
country,total_amount
USA,125.50
Mexico,98.00
  </expected_country_sales_summary_csv>

  <!-- process.log is allowed to vary except that it must be UTF-8, contain at least 
       two lines, and must include the two key substrings. The autograder will grep 
       for “orders_enriched.json created” and “country_sales_summary.csv created”. -->
name: task_000000_cf7c3970
